verses.text: John is given a reed to measure the temple, but the outer court is left, 'given unto the Gentiles,' who tread the city 'forty and two months.' Measured. God marks His own amid trampling.
John is given a reed to measure the temple, but the outer court is left, 'given unto the Gentiles,' who tread the city 'forty and two months.' Measured. God marks His own amid trampling.
'My two witnesses' prophesy in sackcloth, with power to shut heaven and smite the earth, as 'two olive trees.' Witnesses. God's testimony will not be silenced.
'My two witnesses' prophesy in sackcloth, with power to shut heaven and smite the earth, as 'two olive trees.' Witnesses. God's testimony will not be silenced.
'My two witnesses' prophesy in sackcloth, with power to shut heaven and smite the earth, as 'two olive trees.' Witnesses. God's testimony will not be silenced.
'My two witnesses' prophesy in sackcloth, with power to shut heaven and smite the earth, as 'two olive trees.' Witnesses. God's testimony will not be silenced.
When their testimony ends, the beast 'shall make war against them, and... kill them,' their bodies left in the street. Slain witnesses. The world rejoices at their fall.
When their testimony ends, the beast 'shall make war against them, and... kill them,' their bodies left in the street. Slain witnesses. The world rejoices at their fall.
Men gaze on the dead, rejoice, and 'send gifts,' for these prophets tormented them. Cruel joy. The world celebrates the silencing of truth.
Men gaze on the dead, rejoice, and 'send gifts,' for these prophets tormented them. Cruel joy. The world celebrates the silencing of truth.
After three days and a half 'the Spirit of life from God entered into them,' and they rose and ascended in a cloud. Raised. God vindicates His witnesses before all.
After three days and a half 'the Spirit of life from God entered into them,' and they rose and ascended in a cloud. Raised. God vindicates His witnesses before all.
A great earthquake slays seven thousand; the rest, afraid, 'gave glory to the God of heaven.' The second woe is past. Awe. Judgment turns some at last to fear God.
A great earthquake slays seven thousand; the rest, afraid, 'gave glory to the God of heaven.' The second woe is past. Awe. Judgment turns some at last to fear God.
The seventh trumpet: 'The kingdoms of this world are become the kingdoms of our Lord, and of his Christ; and he shall reign for ever.' He reigns. Christ takes His everlasting kingdom.
The seventh trumpet: 'The kingdoms of this world are become the kingdoms of our Lord, and of his Christ; and he shall reign for ever.' He reigns. Christ takes His everlasting kingdom.
The seventh trumpet: 'The kingdoms of this world are become the kingdoms of our Lord, and of his Christ; and he shall reign for ever.' He reigns. Christ takes His everlasting kingdom.
The nations were angry, but Your wrath is come, to judge and reward; the temple opens, showing the ark. Reward and judgment. God repays His servants and judges the rest.
The nations were angry, but Your wrath is come, to judge and reward; the temple opens, showing the ark. Reward and judgment. God repays His servants and judges the rest.
